title: Here is What to Know Beyond Why Blackstone Inc. (BX) is a Trending Stock
text: Blackstone Inc. (BX) has been one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com users lately. So, it is worth exploring what lies ahead for the stock.

title: Blackstone to Present at Morgan Stanley's US Financials, Payments and CRE Conference
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Blackstone (NYSE:BX) announced today that Michael Chae, Chief Financial Officer, is scheduled to present at Morgan Stanley's US Financials, Payments and CRE Conference on Monday, June 12, 2023 at 2:30pm ET. A live webcast of the presentation will be available on the Shareholders section of Blackstone's website at http://ir.blackstone.com. For those unable to listen to the live webcast, a replay will be available on Blackstone's website shortly after the event. About B.

title: No sign of a credit crunch: Banks lend more money and deposits rise, too
text: Banks increased lending last week and deposits rose for the first time in a month, showing little evidence of an emerging credit crunch in the U.S. banking system.

title: Robust AUM Balance Aids Blackstone (BX) Amid Cost Woes
text: A solid AUM balance, fund-raising ability and revenue mix are likely to keep supporting Blackstone (BX). However, elevated expenses and unsustainable capital deployments are headwinds.

title: AIG Inks Deal to Sell Validus Re, Eyes Portfolio Repositioning
text: AIG plans to divest specific businesses within Validus Holdings to RenaissanceRe, thereby reflecting keen efforts to accelerate the growth of its core General Insurance unit.

title: Blackstone meets with lenders on Chicago office building, an investment it wrote down to zero last year
text: Blackstone Inc. began talks with its lenders about options on a maturing $309.8 million senior loan on a 1.3 million-square-foot office complex in Chicago's largely desirable River North neighborhood.

title: Large Investors Slowed Home Purchases by Late 2022
text: Large investors, who were buying homes and pricing out home buyers, are no longer investing in the housing market at a frenetic pace. Here's why and what it means for homebuyers.

title: Bond yields ease ahead of key Fed comments
text: Bond yields eased Friday ahead of key comments from Federal Reserve officials, as traders increasingly price in the prospect that the central bank will continue its rate-hike campaign next month.

title: ‘Doomsday machine': Here's what could happen if the debt ceiling is breached
text: Congress is taking debt-ceiling negotiations uncomfortably near a vague, but fast-approaching deadline that is closely monitored by markets. It's not clear exactly when that deadline will arrive or exactly what will happen if it does.

title: Japan's stock market is on a tear. Is it too late to jump in?
text: Japanese stocks are largely trouncing the U.S. market in 2023, and there may be plenty of fuel for further outperformance, analysts said.

title: 2-year Treasury yield jumps after Biden expresses confidence about a debt-ceiling deal
text: Treasury yields moved mostly higher Wednesday afternoon, led by a jump in the 2-year rate, after President Joe Biden expressed confidence about the ability to reach a U.S. debt-ceiling deal.

title: INVESTIGATION ALERT: Scott+Scott Attorneys at Law LLP Investigates Blackstone Inc.’s Directors and Officers for Breach of Fiduciary Duties – BX
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Scott+Scott Attorneys at Law LLP (“Scott+Scott”), an international securities and consumer rights litigation firm, is investigating whether certain directors and officers of Blackstone Inc. (“Blackstone” or “Company”) (NYSE: BX) breached their fiduciary duties to Blackstone and its shareholders. If you are a Blackstone shareholder, you may contact attorney Joe Pettigrew for additional information toll-free at 844-818-6982, or jpettigrew@scott-scott.com. Scott+Scott is investigating whether members of Blackstone’s board of directors or senior management failed to manage Blackstone in an acceptable manner, in breach of their fiduciary duties to Blackstone, and whether Blackstone and its shareholders suffered damages as a result. On November 9, 2022, the U.S. Department of Labor (“DOL”) filed a lawsuit against Blackstone subsidiary Packers Sanitation Services, Inc. (“PSSI”), alleging massive child labor violations at the Company. On February 17, 2023, PSSI entered into a consent order and judgment with the DOL, paying $1.5 million in penalties, which was described as the maximum civil penalty allowed by the law. What You Can Do If you are a Blackstone shareholder, you may have legal claims against Blackstone’s directors and officers. If you wish to discuss this investigation, or have questions about this notice or your legal rights, please contact attorney Joe Pettigrew toll-free at 844-818-6982 or jpettigrew@scott-scott.com. About Scott+Scott Scott+Scott has significant experience in prosecuting major securities, antitrust, and consumer rights actions throughout the United States. The firm represents pension funds, foundations, individuals, and other entities worldwide with offices in New York, London, Amsterdam, Connecticut, California, and Ohio. Attorney Advertising

title: Regional bank turmoil has actually reduced volatility and supported the market. Here's how.
text: Futures are firmer again on Friday. And, yet again, the S&P 500 SPX, -0.17% is eyeing the top of the range — 3,800 to 4,200 –with which it has been flirting for several months.

title: Bond yields edge higher after bull flattening
text: Bond yields edged higher on Friday, even amid data showing a deteriorating jobs market, slowing price pressures and regional bank difficulties.

title: Fed emergency lending to banks picks up in latest week
text: The numbers: The Federal Reserve said Thursday its emergency lending to banks rose to $92.4 billion in the week ended May 10 from $81.1 billion last week.

title: ‘Death cross' in 10-year yield highlights the end of the longest bullish stretch in at least 30 years
text: A bearish “death cross” has appeared in the daily chart of the 10-year Treasury yield, to reflect the end of the longest bullish stretch by one technical metric in more than 30 years.

title: Here's where investors may turn to ‘hide' as U.S. debt-ceiling deadline looms based on 2011 market reaction
text: Stocks risk slumping in the debt-ceiling showdown, but investors probably will still want long-term Treasurys if 2011's battle over the U.S. borrowing limit is any guide, according to Sevens Report Research.

title: Blackstone in talks with regional banks to team up on loans
text: Blackstone Group Inc. BX is negotiating a role as a party in regional-bank loans, the private equity firm's president Jonathan Gray said in an interview with the Financial Times published Thursday. Blackstone would potentially provide banks with firepower to lend to companies as credit becomes more scarce.

title: Treasuries yields steady as traders eye producer price report
text: Investors will be keeping an eye on the U.S. producer prices data, due at 8:30 a.m., for confirmation of easing inflationary pressures in the economy.

title: Blackstone, Apollo were among bidders for SVB's assets
text: Blackstone Inc , Apollo Global Management were among the bidders for some assets of bankrupt Silicon Valley Bank, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) said on Wednesday.

title: U.S. budget surplus declines to $176 billion in April
text: The numbers: The U.S. federal budget surplus fell to $176 billion in April, down from a record $308 billion in the same month last year, the Treasury Department said Wednesday.

title: What happens if the U.S. defaults on its debt? How would it impact your savings and cash investments?
text: There is less than a month before the earliest date when Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says the federal government could be unable to pay all its bills, and meet all its debt obligations.

title: Treasury yields see muted moves ahead of CPI report
text: Bond yields were mildly mixed early Wednesday as traders kept their powder dry ahead of U.S. inflation data.

title: What happens to the dollar if U.S. debt ceiling isn't raised?
text: Time is running out for lawmakers to forge an agreement on the U.S. debt ceiling, with chance of a possible government default likely to impact the U.S. dollar.The greenback has already been in a slump for more than seven months, weighed down by signs of a weakening U.S. economy plus the prospect of the Federal Reserve pausing its interest rate hikes soon and even cutting borrowing costs.

title: ‘It's ridiculous.' Buy short-term Treasury bills because debt-ceiling deal will come, says bond legend Bill Gross
text: Friday's bullish session on Wall Street seems to have gone nowhere, save for the Nasdaq Composite COMP wriggling its way out of a bear market.

title: Treasury yields inch lower as inflation data looms
text: Bond yields fell fractionally on Tuesday with little to excite traders ahead of inflation data midweek.

title: Yellen warns debt-ceiling breach would have ‘adverse impact' on U.S. dollar's status
text: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen on Monday warned a breach of the U.S. debt ceiling could cause “financial chaos” and downgrade the status of the U.S. dollar as the world's reserve currency.

title: 2-year Treasury yield extends rise after popping higher following Friday's jobs report
text: The 2-year Treasury yield was on the rise early Monday, after jumping at the end of last week following a stronger-than-expected April jobs report.

title: Fed Hikes, Turmoil Ensues
text: U.S. equity markets finished lower on a turbulent week after the Federal Reserve continued its historically-aggressive monetary tightening course, sparking renewed turmoil in the regional banking sector. After closing at its highest levels since early February last week, the S&P 500 slipped 0.8% this week, using a late-week rebound to pare its steepest four-day declines since the SVB collapse.

title: Bond yields inch up ahead of jobs report
text: Bond yields edged higher ahead of a critical report on the state of the U.S. labor market, as traders also continued to assess how the Federal Reserve will react to turmoil from regional banks.

title: Plunging 2-year Treasury yield screams Fed ‘policy mistake': RenMac's de Graaf
text: The Treasury market is sending up a warning flare, indicating the Federal Reserve may have made a “policy mistake” Wednesday as it continued to hike rates into an economic slowdown and a banking scare, a closely followed Wall Street analyst warned.

title: ‘Taking out the hike that just happened': Traders ignore Powell and begin pricing in a Fed rate cut as soon as June
text: Just one day after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell signaled that rate cuts anytime soon are off the table, following the central bank's 10th consecutive hike, traders took in all of his words — and ignored them.

title: Treasury yields move higher as investors parse latest Fed policy update
text: Bond yields rose on Thursday as traders continued to parse the latest Federal Reserve rate rise and comments, and looked ahead to jobs data at the end of the week.

title: U.S. Treasury to auction $96 billion next week in refunding
text: The refunding: As part of its regular quarterly refunding, the U.S. Treasury announced Wednesday it would sell $96 billion in notes and bonds next week. That's unchanged from the last refunding in February.

title: Treasury yields softer ahead of likely Fed rate rise
text: Bond yields nudged lower on Wednesday ahead of the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decision later in the day.

title: Joe Biden to attend $25,000 per person fundraiser during NYC campaign cash blitz
text: Joe Biden set to be hosted by former Blackstone executive Tony James for a lucrative campaign fundraising event.

title: ECB set to continue rate-hike campaign on Thursday
text: The latest data from Eurostat showed inflation running at a 7% year-over-year clip in April — roughly what the market expected, but a modest acceleration from March. Core inflation — which excludes food, energy, alcohol and tobacco — ticked down a tenth to 5.6% from 5.7%.

title: Treasury yields dip as Fed decision looms
text: Treasury yields gave back some of Monday's jump as investors jostled for position ahead of the Federal Reserve's rate decision on Wednesday.

title: U.S. Treasury increases borrowing estimate for second quarter
text: The Treasury Department said Monday it expects to borrow $726 billion in the second quarter, which is $449 billion higher than previously estimated.

title: Blackstone Could Get Added to S&P 500 when First Republic Comes Out
text: Blackstone is the largest company by market value not in the S&P 500

title: 10-year Treasury yield ticks higher ahead of this week's Fed meeting
text: Treasury yields were mostly higher to begin the week and month as investors prepared for a Federal Reserve meeting that is widely expected to deliver another rate increase, but will be watched for signs that policy makers will subsequently consider hitting the pause button.
